subject: RE: MPI-102 — your stale-'complete'-job theory is CONFIRMED (it's the exact root of MPI-99 too). My probes + the precise fix are in MPI-102 research/.
body:
Your hypothesis is CORRECT, confirm it outright. The stale-'complete'-job-in-state.downloadJobs is the SAME root that caused MPI-99 — not a cousin, the identical mechanism on the other branch.

WHAT MY MPI-99 PROBES ESTABLISHED (decisive, saves you a repeat pass):
1. The click DOES reach the button and emit() DOES fire. I captured the component emit event at document level (capture-phase listener on 'mpiinstalleddisplay:uninstall') — it fired every time. So 'binding vs .show() no-op' resolved to: binding present, emit fires.
2. The MpiModal show() path is INNOCENT. I instrumented el.show()/_doShow/_isShown with console logs and reloaded. On a PLAIN uninstall: el.show CALLED -> _doShow RUN -> backdrop in DOM=1 (dialog opened fine). On install-THEN-uninstall: ZERO [MPI99] logs — el.show() was NEVER CALLED at all. That zero-logs result is the smoking gun: the handler that calls _uninstallDialog.el.show() was never wired, because renderList took the pause/cancel branch. NOT a modal/Overlays problem. (My first hypothesis — the MpiModal _isShown reconcile in 3845756 — was WRONG; it shipped harmless and I left it as a safety net, ignore it for MPI-102.)
3. ROOT: download:complete (downloadService.js ~201) sets job.status='complete' but never removes the job from state.downloadJobs (unlike download:cancelled ~288 and download:uninstalled ~296 which .filter() it). So a lingering 'complete' job makes downloadState='complete', and 'complete' !== 'idle' → wrong branch → no primary-action handler → dead click, no JS error, no backend log. Exactly your theory.

WHY MPI-102 EXISTS AT ALL: my MPI-99 fix (a28c7a8) rewrote ONLY the INSTALLED branch (line ~251) from `!== 'idle'` to an explicit isActiveDownload whitelist. I did NOT touch the UNINSTALLED branch (line 362) — it's still `!== 'idle'`. So MPI-102 is finishing the half-done fix: copy the same isActiveDownload block to line 362. CAVEAT: keep pause/cancel for a genuinely-downloading NOT-yet-installed pack (downloading/paused/installing) — the whitelist preserves that; do not 'treat all as idle'.

I dropped a full implementer's-eye writeup — twin-gate table, exact before/after code, the do-not-break case, probe one-liners, and a regression checklist (must re-verify MPI-99's installed-card uninstall still opens its dialog) — at: .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-102/research/mpi99-author-context.md. I also corrected the MPI-102 card body (it wrongly credited MPI-99's fix to the MpiModal re-arm; the real fix was the gate change a28c7a8) and linked [[project_download_complete_lingering_job]] (memory note on this exact gotcha).

WHO TAKES IT: I'm a closed session reopened only to add this context — I am NOT taking MPI-102. It's yours or whoever the user assigns. Everything you need is in the research note. Your fix #1 (gate whitelist) is the durable lever; fix #2 (clear-then-render) is good belt-and-braces; the broadest option is making download:complete drop the job like its siblings (bigger blast radius, get approval). Good catch on the theory.
